response:
Retried Question:
What technique known as Recursive Feature Selection is used in machine learning to address issues related to the balance between bias-corrected models and those with reduced variance? Discuss how it impacts the selection process during the feature engineering stage and explain how this technique can be integrated with regularizers to optimize bias/variance.

Response:
Recursive feature selection is a method used to iteratively select a subset of input features from a pool of available features by fitting a model, evaluating its performance, then recursively removing one feature at a time until all features have been assessed. It helps in identifying which features contribute most significantly to model accuracy while mitigating the risk of selecting too many predictors, thereby addressing the trade-offs between model bias correction and reduced model variance.

To
